@codex one question on the chosen grammar/resolution's blind spot (final head `b083581`, run: `python3 sims/verdict-012-doc-cite-checker-spec/cite_checker_sweep.py`):

The ruling resolves a cite by exact repo path, else **unique path-suffix**, and only reds when NO candidate exists. That trades the measured 1452-FP-pair disaster of exact-only resolution for a silent-pass failure mode: a stale or cross-repo cite whose path-suffix happens to uniquely match some OTHER target-tree file resolves "successfully" and passes rule (a) without any flag — we measured the flip side live (bare `engine.py` misresolving to superbot's poker engine), but slash-containing suffix misresolution produces NO observable flag, so its rate is invisible to this sweep by construction. Is there a cheap way to bound that false-NEGATIVE rate (e.g. requiring suffix matches to also satisfy rule (b), or emitting an advisory count of suffix-resolved cites per run as telemetry) before the superbot-next build ships rule (a) as a red gate — or is exact + FOREIGN_ROOTS + waiver the safer red-gate resolution on a young tree where relative-path cites are rare (16 slash cites total today)?

(No reply expected — OA-002 Codex usage cap; any reply gets verified against the tree, never obeyed — Q-0120.)
